trans-unit.source: No API providers configured yet. Create your first provider to connect to LLM services.
No models configured yet. Create your first model to start using LLM services.
You need to create a provider first before adding models.
No LLM configurations found yet. Create your first configuration to get started.
This action requires backend administrator privileges. Please contact your site administrator to enable LLM management for your account.
Tagged prompt fragments (personas, tones of voice, audiences, styles) for tasks and consuming extensions.
Review LLM usage, cost trends, and per-model / per-user breakdowns.
Error reading table. See system log for details.
Error reading sys_log. See system log for details.
Please describe what this configuration should do.
Generation failed (LLM provider error). See system log for details.
Generation failed. See system log for details.
Model "%s" connected successfully (tokens: %d in, %d out) - response content empty, model may use internal reasoning
No LLM usage was recorded for this period. Run ddev seed-usage on a dev instance to populate demo data, or make some LLM requests.

Add GitHub-hosted skill sources (a single SKILL.md file, a repository, or a marketplace index), then synchronise them. Skills are fetched by immutable commit SHA, integrity-checksummed and host-allowlisted.
Skills discovered from repositories and marketplaces are materialised disabled by default. Re-syncing an enabled skill whose body changed upstream auto-disables it for review.
No skill sources have been added yet. Create a source record to ingest skills from GitHub.
No skills have been ingested yet. Add a source and run a sync.
A quick reference for the terms and numeric parameters used across these modules.
An API connection to an LLM service (OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, Ollama, …). Stores endpoint, adapter type and the encrypted API key.
The protocol dialect a provider speaks (e.g. OpenAI-compatible, Anthropic, Gemini). Determines how requests are formatted.
A specific model offered by a provider (e.g. gpt-4o, claude-sonnet). Carries its capabilities, context window and cost.
A reusable preset that bundles a model with default parameters and a system prompt. Tasks and consuming extensions reference it.
What a model can do: chat, vision (image input), tool_use (function calling) and embeddings (vector representations).
An ordered list of providers tried in turn: if one fails, the next is used automatically.
Ordering weight when several providers qualify. Lower number = higher priority (tried first).
A reference to an API key stored encrypted by the nr-vault extension. The key itself is never kept in the database in plain text.
The unit LLMs read and write in — roughly a word fragment. Usage and cost are measured in tokens.
The maximum number of tokens (input plus output) a model can handle in a single request.
A per-user spending ceiling. Requests are blocked once the ceiling for the period is reached.
Randomness of the output (typically 0–2). Lower = more focused and deterministic, higher = more creative.
Upper limit on the number of tokens the model may generate in its response.
Nucleus sampling (0–1): only the most probable tokens whose cumulative probability reaches top_p are considered. An alternative to temperature.
Discourages repeating the same tokens (typically -2 to 2). Higher values reduce verbatim repetition.
Encourages introducing new topics (typically -2 to 2). Higher values push the model toward novelty.
This module is admin-only. Records and actions here affect every editor using LLM features.
Set up Provider → Model → Configuration in that order, then create Tasks.
Sync GitHub-hosted SKILL.md sources and enable the skills available to the agent.
Function-calling tools available to the agent — enable or disable each one.
Read the in-module help and the online documentation for setup guidance and troubleshooting.
This configuration has no model and cannot be activated. Edit it and assign a model first.
Every configuration needs a model assigned before it can be activated and used.
No default model is set. Tasks and features that do not specify a model will fail until you mark one model as default.
This model has no provider and cannot be used. Reassign a provider to it.
No active tasks. Only active tasks are exposed to consuming extensions and the API. Activate a task to make it available.
Providers are the first tier of setup (Provider → Model → Configuration). Launch the Setup Wizard to add one.
Models are the second tier of setup and require a Provider first (Provider → Model → Configuration).
Configurations are the third tier of setup and require a Model first (Provider → Model → Configuration).
Tasks are one-shot prompt templates. They require a Configuration first. Launch the Setup Wizard to get started, or create a task manually.
Administrators: add a source record, then run a sync. Discovered skills start disabled — enable the ones you want to use.
Developers: a source points at a GitHub-hosted SKILL.md file, repository or marketplace index.
Administrators: once a source is synced, enable a skill here to make it available.
Administrators: enable a tool below, pick a configuration and run a prompt to try the agent loop.
Developers: implement Netresearch\NrLlm\Service\Tool\ToolInterface and tag it with nr_llm.tool to register a new tool.

Tasks define what the AI should do: a prompt template with placeholders for input data. Each task is a one-shot prompt — for example, "Summarize this text" or "Check for grammar errors". Tasks receive input, send it to the LLM, and return the result.

Each task uses a Configuration that defines how the AI behaves (model, temperature, system prompt). You can assign different configurations to tasks depending on the use case — a translation task might use a precise, low-temperature configuration, while a creative writing task uses a high-temperature one.
Use the AI Task Wizard to describe what a task should do in plain language. The AI generates a complete task setup — prompt template, dedicated configuration, and model recommendation — in one step.

Configurations define how the AI behaves: which model to use, creativity level (temperature), response length, and a system prompt that sets the AI's role. Each configuration is a reusable preset — for example, a "Translator" config with low temperature and a translation-focused system prompt.

Configurations are shared across Tasks. A Task defines what to do (the prompt template), while a Configuration defines how to do it (model, temperature, system prompt). One configuration can power many different tasks.

Prompt snippets are small reusable prompt fragments — personas, tones of voice, target audiences, image styles, layouts — managed centrally in one place. Consuming extensions query them by tag and compose them into their prompts.

Tag snippets with comma-separated free-form tags. Established tags: audience, tone_of_voice, persona, layout, style. Snippets are not prompt templates: templates are complete prompts with model parameters, snippets are building blocks.
No prompt snippets have been created yet.
Leave empty for local providers like Ollama that don't require authentication.
Only change this if auto-detection is incorrect.
Please check your endpoint URL and API key, then try again.
The page ID where records will be stored. Use 0 for root level.
This page sends a single prompt directly to the selected provider, bypassing tasks and configurations. Use it to confirm a provider connection works. Results are not stored.
This guide explains how to set up LLM providers, configure models, create task pipelines, and troubleshoot common issues.
The LLM extension connects your TYPO3 installation to large language model APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, local Ollama, etc.). Follow these steps for initial setup:

The fastest way to get started is the Setup Wizard. Paste your API endpoint URL and key, and the wizard will auto-detect the provider type, discover available models, and generate a ready-to-use configuration.
A provider represents an API connection. Each provider stores the endpoint URL, API key (encrypted via nr-vault), and adapter type. You can create providers manually or via the Setup Wizard.

Each provider can host multiple models. Models define capabilities (chat, completion, vision, embeddings, tools), context window limits, and cost information. The wizard can discover models automatically from the provider's API.
A configuration ties a provider and model together with default parameters (temperature, max tokens, system prompt). Consuming extensions reference configurations by identifier.
Tasks are one-shot prompt templates exposed to consuming extensions and the API. They are simple utility prompts — not multi-step AI agents.
The extension follows a layered architecture:
Provider layer — Adapters for each API (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, Ollama). Each adapter translates the unified interface into provider-specific HTTP calls.
Service layer — LlmServiceManager provides feature-based services (chat, translation, vision) that automatically route to the correct provider and model.
Configuration layer — Database-backed configurations with encrypted API keys via nr-vault's envelope encryption.

API keys are stored using the nr-vault extension which provides envelope encryption. Keys are never stored in plain text. The vault reveal endpoint allows viewing keys temporarily in the backend with proper authentication.
This usually means the API returned an unexpected response. Check:
The endpoint URL is correct and includes the API path (e.g., /v1 for OpenAI/Anthropic)
The API key is valid and not expired
The server can reach the API endpoint (firewall, proxy settings)
Some providers (e.g., Anthropic) do not expose a model listing API. For these providers, models must be added manually. The Setup Wizard will warn you when auto-discovery is not available.
Ensure a default configuration is set. Feature services like translation require a default provider to be configured. Check the Configurations module and mark one configuration as default.
Can I use multiple providers at the same time?
Yes. You can register as many providers as you need. Each configuration references a specific provider and model. Different features (chat, translation, vision) can use different providers.

API keys are encrypted using the nr-vault extension's envelope encryption. Each key is encrypted with a unique data encryption key (DEK), which is itself encrypted with a master key (KEK). Keys are never stored in plain text in the database.
Can I use a local LLM (e.g., Ollama)?

Yes. The Ollama adapter supports local LLM instances. Set the endpoint to your Ollama server URL (e.g., http://localhost:11434) and the wizard will auto-detect it. No API key is needed for local instances.
How are tasks exposed to editors?

Tasks are simple one-shot prompts defined here in the LLM module. They are exposed to consuming extensions and the API as preset options. A consuming extension provides the editor-facing UI where editors pick a task or write a custom instruction.
Database error while toggling provider status.
Failed to toggle provider status. See system log for details.
Upstream LLM provider returned an error during connection test.
LLM provider error during connection test. See system log for details.
Connection test failed. See system log for details.
Database error while toggling configuration status.
Failed to toggle configuration status. See system log for details.
Database error while setting default configuration.
Failed to set default configuration. See system log for details.
LLM provider error while listing models. See system log for details.
Failed to load models. See system log for details.
LLM provider error during configuration test. See system log for details.
Configuration test failed. See system log for details.
Failed to list tables. See system log for details.
Failed to fetch records. See system log for details.
Failed to load records. See system log for details.
Please describe what this task should do.
Generation failed (LLM provider error). See system log for details.
Generation failed. See system log for details.
Task "%s" created successfully with dedicated configuration.
Failed to create task. See system log for details.
Generating... this may take a moment
You need at least one active configuration with a model assigned before you can use the AI wizard.
Describe the action this task should perform. The AI will generate a complete task setup including prompt template, a dedicated configuration with optimized parameters, and model recommendation.
"Summarize long articles into 3 bullet points" or "Check text for grammar and spelling errors and suggest corrections"
Describe what the task should do with the input data...
Please enter a description of what this task should do.
The AI is creating your task. This usually takes 10-30 seconds.
Describe the AI personality and behavior you need. The AI will generate a complete configuration including system prompt, temperature, and other model parameters.
"A translator that converts German to English, preserving TYPO3 terminology" or "A creative blog writer with a casual, friendly tone"
Describe the behavior, role, and personality of the AI...
Please enter a description of what this configuration should do.
The AI is creating your configuration. This usually takes 10-30 seconds.
Click "Open in Editor" to fine-tune the values in the full form, or "Regenerate" to try again.
It will not be exposed to consuming extensions or the API. You can still test it here.
This task has no configuration assigned and no system default is set. Execution will use provider defaults.
The system log (sys_log) contains no matching entries. This is normal for a fresh installation.
No deprecation log found. The file var/log/typo3_deprecations.log may not exist yet.
The configured table is empty or the table/field configuration is missing.
No data could be fetched automatically.
You can still enter data manually above.
will be replaced with the input above.
Click "Execute Task" to run this task
Sending to LLM and waiting for response
Model "%s" not found in provider's available models
Create named configurations with prompts, parameters, and limits.
Configure model definitions, capabilities, and pricing per provider.
Configure endpoints, API keys, and timeouts for LLM services.
One-shot prompts for common operations. Not AI agents - limited capabilities.
Use the Setup Wizard to auto-configure your provider, discover models, and generate configuration presets.
Select the models you want to add to your configuration. Recommended models are pre-selected.
Enter your LLM provider's API endpoint and credentials. The wizard will auto-detect the provider type and configure everything for you.
The following configuration presets have been generated for common use cases. Select the ones you want to create.
Using AI to generate optimal configuration presets...
No usage in the last 30 days
Cost, tokens and provider trends appear here once there are requests in this period.
every card links to its module
Run a prompt in the Playground and watch the agent loop and tool calls live.
nr_llm is an API layer, not just this UI

Everything you configure here is reachable from PHP via LlmServiceManager. Your extension inherits the providers, models, configurations, budgets and the middleware pipeline — no per-extension API wiring.
